title: Convergent ordered-sequence field-kind — CRDT-backed ordered item-refs that merge deterministically under concurrent insert/delete/move
description: A declared sequence-field kind over item-refs: a non-interleaving Fugue-class sequence CRDT (dense, immutable per-element position identifiers) plus a Kleppmann move-CRDT position register resolved by hybrid logical clock, with a sort-on-read projection to a plain ordered ref-array and array-position hand-edits translated to CRDT ops. CRDT state lives in the owning <block>.json; projection + field-aware merge live in block-api. One field-kind plugin into the substrate merge driver (FEAT-002). Per DEC-0005.
acceptance_criteria: Two divergent versions each insert, delete, and/or move elements in the same collection; the merge yields one replica-independent sequence containing all inserts, with no element duplicated or lost and no interleaving of concurrently-inserted runs
Insert mints one position identifier and mutates no neighbor
Move rewrites one element's position register; concurrent moves of the same element resolve deterministically by hybrid logical clock
The sort-on-read projection reproduces single-writer authoring order identically to a plain array, and accepts array-position hand-edits translated to CRDT operations
